breakClass: .ToList()/.ToArray() (or a nested correlated-collection projection) embedded inside a server-side EF Core 10 query â€” either in a `let` clause whose value is consumed by a server `select new {...}`/`where ... .Contains(...)`, or as an inline `Field = (from ... let X=db....ToList() ... select ...).ToList()` sub-collection â€” on an outer IQueryable that has NOT yet crossed an .AsEnumerable()/.ToList() materialization boundary. EF6 client-evaluated these; EF Core 10 throws "could not be translated" (500). Outer queries that are materialized first (var data2 = v.ToList(); from o in data2 ...) or sourced from db.X.AsEnumerable() are SAFE.
why: Both the `amc` (from db.Amcs, let assign line 370) and `task` (from db.ProTasks, let assign line 392) queries source from un-materialized IQueryables; let assign = db....ToList() is consumed by where assign.Contains(EmpId), forcing the embedded .ToList() server-side. EF Core 10 cannot translate it. Port regression. (Two breaks in this method.)
finalFix:
Replace only the WHERE-clause usage of `assign.Contains(EmpId)` with a translatable correlated `Any(...)`, mirroring legacy output exactly.

amc query (lines 366-387) â€” drop the `let assign` (line 370) entirely (projection never uses it) and change line 374:
  (editperiodic==true || assign.Contains(EmpId))
to:
  (editperiodic==true || db.PeriodicMaintAssignedToes.Any(x => x.PeriodicMaintDtlId == d.PeriodicMaintDetailsId && x.Status == "Assigned" && x.ChkStatus == Status.active && x.EmployeeId == EmpId))

task query (lines 388-417) â€” KEEP `let assign` (line 392) because the projection at line 407 emits `assign` for client-side use after .ToList() (line 419). Change only line 396:
  (editprotask==true || assign.Contains(EmpId))
to:
  (editprotask==true || db.TaskAssigneds.Any(x => x.ProTaskId == a.ProTaskId && x.Status == "Assigned" && x.chkStatus == Status.active && x.EmployeeId == EmpId))

Both rewrites are output-identical (Any over the same predicate set + EmployeeId==EmpId equals "the materialized EmployeeId list contains EmpId") and translate to a clean SQL EXISTS subquery.

Also note (out of audit scope but identical break class in the same method): the `periodictask` query at line 421 has the same `let assign = db.TaskAssigneds.Where(...).ToList()` + `assign.Contains(EmpId)` in its WHERE (lines 423/427) and will 500 the same way; apply the same Any(...) rewrite there. And the parallel ProTaskNonTechController.cs (legacy lines 222/244/275) carries identical patterns.
reasoning:
I read QuickNetCore/Controllers/ProTaskController.cs (method mapviewshow, lines 304-442) and compared it byte-for-byte against _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/ProTaskController.cs:363-417. They are identical, so this is a faithful port and any break is a regression.

(a) SERVER-SIDE â€” CONFIRMED. Both flagged queries are built directly on DbSets with NO prior materialization boundary:
- amc (line 366): `from a in db.Amcs join ... join d in db.PeriodicMaintenanceDetails ... let assign = db.PeriodicMaintAssignedToes.Where(...).Select(x=>x.EmployeeId).ToList() where ... (editperiodic==true||assign.Contains(EmpId)) ... select new points {...}).ToList()`. The terminal `.ToList()` at line 387 is the FIRST materialization point; the `let assign` (line 370) and its consumer `assign.Contains(EmpId)` (line 374) sit inside the still-IQueryable outer tree.
- task (line 388): `from a in db.ProTasks.Where(o=>o.Branch==1) join c ... join am ... from am in amcd.DefaultIfEmpty() let assign = db.TaskAssigneds.Where(...).Select(x=>x.EmployeeId).ToList() where ... (editprotask==true||assign.Contains(EmpId)) ... select new {...}).ToList().Select(...)`. First materialization is the `.ToList()` at line 410; the `let assign` (392) and `assign.Contains(EmpId)` (396) are server-side. Neither is after .AsEnumerable()/.ToList(), neither is a standalone top-level query, neither is a group-aggregate over an IGrouping.

(b) WOULD 500 â€” CONFIRMED. EF Core 10 must translate the whole outer query to SQL. A `.ToList()` materialization embedded in the expression tree, with the resulting List consumed by `assign.Contains(EmpId)` in the WHERE, is untranslatable â†’ InvalidOperationException "The LINQ expression ... could not be translated" â†’ HTTP 500. EF6 client-evaluated this and ran fine (legacy is identical). Note the `||` does not save it: EF Core fails at translation time, not at row-evaluation time, so even editperiodic/editprotask==true requests throw. High severity: also reached on realistic data for users lacking the 'AMC CREATE' / 'Edit ProTask' roles. Two distinct breaks in one method (lines 370 and 392), matching the hunter's claim.

I did NOT alter the hunter's task-query nuance incorrectly: the task projection (line 407) still emits `assign` into the anonymous type for client-side use after .ToList() (line 419 re-queries db.Employees from o.assign), so the fix must keep producing `assign` and only replace the WHERE-clause usage. amc's projection does not reference assign, so the `let` can be dropped entirely there.

method: SaveServiceReport (returns prdata JSON; the printdata query)
breakClass: Nested collection / sub-entity projection EF Core 10 cannot translate: an inline nested `Field = (from x in db.Y join ... select new {...}).ToList()` (or a `let img = db.Y.Where(...).Select(...).ToList()` that is then projected) placed inside a SERVER-SIDE outer `select new {...}` that is enumerated by `.ToList()`/`.FirstOrDefault()` with no prior `.AsEnumerable()`/`.ToList()` boundary. EF6 ran these (separate per-row queries / query-splitting); EF Core 10 throws `The LINQ expression ... could not be translated` / `ProjectionBindingExpression could not be translated` -> HTTP 500. Two confirmed systemic sub-classes: (A) Common.cs PDF/print builders projecting img + bundle (+inner bimg) collections; (B) process-flow/status/service-report master grids projecting AssignType/AssignedUsers/Depts/Desgn/contacts/itemsused collections. NOTE: the HireReturn/CrossHireReturn/PackingList pdfItem builders and the MyReports/Leads/ProTask *dashboards* are SAFE because the outer source is already materialized (`from o in v` after `.ToList()`, or nested `.ToList()` sits after `.Distinct().ToList().Select(...)`), making the nested query a client-side N+1, not a 500.
why: Server-side query over db.ProTasks terminated by .FirstOrDefault() at line 3330 (single row, still server-side - NO materialization boundary). The select new projects three nested collections (contacts, AssignedTo, itemsused) via inline (from ... select new {...}).ToList(). EF Core 10 cannot translate these collection projections -> 500 when saving/printing a service report.
reasoning:
CONFIRMED â€” the hunter's flag is correct. The query at QuickNetCore/Controllers/ProTaskController.cs line 3222 lives inside the POST action ServiceReport(ServiceReportViewModel vmodel) (declared line 2927; the orchestrator's label "SaveServiceReport" is a misnomer but the location is right).

(a) SERVER-SIDE â€” positively confirmed. `printdata` is composed directly from `db.ProTasks.Where(o=>o.Branch==1)` (line 3222) and enumerated by `.FirstOrDefault()` at line 3330. There is NO `.ToList()`/`.AsEnumerable()`/`.ToArray()` boundary on that source between lines 3219 and 3222 (only scalar locals `uid`/`mymc` precede it). The three nested `.ToList()` collections (contacts 3251-3300, AssignedTo 3301-3312, itemsused 3313-3328) sit INSIDE the outer server-side `select new {...}`, so the whole projection tree is handed to EF Core's SQL translator as one expression. `.FirstOrDefault()` is NOT a client boundary â€” EF Core 10 must translate the entire projection (including the three correlated/local-filtered collection subqueries) before it can emit SQL or fetch any row. It is not a group-aggregate over an IGrouping and not a standalone scalar query. DbSets servicereports/servicereportmembers/itemtasklist/ContactRelation all exist (ApplicationDbContext.cs), so the query is valid and compilable.

(b) WOULD 500 â€” positively confirmed, severity HIGH. This is the exact "nested collection / sub-entity projection inside a server-side select new" class that EF Core 10 cannot translate and throws `The LINQ expression ... could not be translated` -> HTTP 500. This class is not theoretical here: it is already confirmed-and-fixed elsewhere in THIS codebase and live-verified (memory batch 24-26, commit ea4f19c): GetAllTaskdashexp (same file, line 1622) and Leads/leadsnewdashboard were broken by the identical pattern and fixed by materializing the outer set then building sub-collections client-side. Because translation fails before any row is fetched, it 500s on every hit with a valid srid (realistic data, not edge-only).

PORT REGRESSION â€” confirmed. The legacy EF6 source at _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/ProTaskController.cs line 3404 is byte-for-byte identical (same three nested `.ToList()` projections inside the same server-side `select new ... .FirstOrDefault()`). EF6 executed these as separate per-row/correlated queries; EF Core 10 does not. Faithful port, genuine regression.

NOTE (out of scope of this verdict but worth flagging to the orchestrator): the twin method generatepdfservicereport (line 3373) and the other three printdata builders in this file (legacy mirrors at 4034/4276) carry the identical defect.

why: Duplicate of the SaveServiceReport printdata query, used by the PDF generator path (nested collections contacts/AssignedTo/itemsused projected into a server-side .FirstOrDefault()). EF Core 10 cannot translate -> 500 when downloading/emailing a service-report PDF.
reasoning:
CONFIRMED â€” I refuted my own default and the hunter is right. File: QuickNetCore/Controllers/ProTaskController.cs, method generatepdfservicereport, the printdata query spanning lines 3373-3482.

(a) GENUINELY SERVER-SIDE: Lines 3373-3482 are one continuous query expression: `var printdata = (from a in db.ProTasks.Where(o=>o.Branch==1) join b in db.servicereports ... select new { ...scalars..., contacts=(from c in db.Contacts join cr in db.ContactRelation on new{c.ContactID,RelationType=rtask} equals new{cr.ContactID,cr.RelationType} where cr.RelationID==a.ProTaskId select new{...}).ToList(), AssignedTo=(from z in db.servicereportmembers join y in db.Employees ... where z.servicereportid==srid select new{...}).Distinct().ToList(), itemsused=(from aa in db.itemtasklist join ... where aa.protaskid==a.ProTaskId && c.MCId==mymc select new{...}).ToList() }).FirstOrDefault();`. The outer source is the DbSet IQueryable db.ProTasks.Where(...) â€” there is NO .ToList()/.AsEnumerable()/.ToArray() boundary anywhere before the terminal .FirstOrDefault() at line 3482. It is not a standalone scalar query, not a group-aggregate over an IGrouping. The three nested .ToList() collection projections are composed into the server-side outer select. Server-side confirmed.

(b) PORT REGRESSION vs EF6: The legacy EF6 source at _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/ProTaskController.cs lines 3555-3664 is byte-for-byte the identical generatepdfservicereport method with the same printdata query (same joins, same three nested .ToList()/.Distinct().ToList() collections, same .FirstOrDefault()). EF6 executed it via separate per-row collection queries, so the app worked. The port copied it verbatim into EF Core 10. Genuine regression.

(b) WOULD 500: This is the confirmed systemic sub-class (B) from the prior audit (memory reports-audit-batch24-26 / commit ea4f19c). The nested collections carry constructs EF Core 10's correlated-collection translator rejects: contacts uses an anonymous-type composite-key equijoin with a captured constant (new{c.ContactID,RelationType=rtask}) plus outer correlation cr.RelationID==a.ProTaskId; AssignedTo applies .Distinct() inside the correlated projection. EF Core 10 throws "The LINQ expression ... could not be translated" -> HTTP 500. DECISIVE corroboration: the identical-shape sibling GetAllTaskdashexp in this SAME controller (line 1622) was already confirmed broken and rewritten with exactly the materialize-then-lookup fix â€” its original nested AssignedTo collection (Employees join -> LastName/FirstName/MiddleName/Img/Status, .Distinct().ToList()) is the same shape as the AssignedTo here.

REACHABILITY/SEVERITY: HIGH. Download(long id) (line 3341) -> generatePdf(id) (3353) -> generatepdfservicereport(id). Hit on every service-report PDF download/email; 500s as soon as a valid srid is requested with realistic data. The line-3222 SaveServiceReport sibling (same shape, returned as JSON prdata) has the same defect.

breakClass: Nested collection / sub-entity projection EF Core 10 cannot translate: an inline nested `Field = (from x in db.Y join ... select new {...}).ToList()` (or a `let img = db.Y.Where(...).Select(...).ToList()` that is then projected) placed inside a SERVER-SIDE outer `select new {...}` that is enumerated by `.ToList()`/`.FirstOrDefault()` with no prior `.AsEnumerable()`/`.ToList()` boundary. EF6 ran these (separate per-row queries / query-splitting); EF Core 10 throws `The LINQ expression ... could not be translated` / `ProjectionBindingExpression could not be translated` -> HTTP 500. Two confirmed systemic sub-classes: (A) Common.cs PDF/print builders projecting img + bundle (+inner bimg) collections; (B) process-flow/status/service-report master grids projecting AssignType/AssignedUsers/Depts/Desgn/contacts/itemsused collections. NOTE: the HireReturn/CrossHireReturn/PackingList pdfItem builders and the MyReports/Leads/ProTask *dashboards* are SAFE because the outer source is already materialized (`from o in v` after `.ToList()`, or nested `.ToList()` sits after `.Distinct().ToList().Select(...)`), making the nested query a client-side N+1, not a 500.
why: Server-side query over db.ProTasks enumerated by .ToList() at line 775; the let-bound AssignedTo collection (a (from z ... select new{...}).Distinct().ToList()) is projected into the outer select new at line 772. EF Core 10 cannot translate projecting that nested collection -> 500. Med (not high) because cald is the per-driver calendar view, reached only for a selected driver/date with matching tasks, but it will 500 whenever rows exist. Legacy is identical (port regression).
finalFix:
Mirror the already-accepted batch 24-26 fix from GetAllTaskdashexp: drop the AssignedTo collection from the EF projection, materialize the outer rows, then build the assignee-name string client-side via a lookup.

1) Once, BEFORE the `for` loop (these rows do not depend on the time slot `fdate`; the loop currently re-runs the identical task query 38 times â€” pulling it out also removes 37 redundant round-trips), or alternatively keep it inside the loop but in both cases remove `AssignedTo` from the projection:

   var task = (from a in db.ProTasks.Where(o => o.Branch == 1)
               join e in db.Employees on a.driver equals e.EmployeeId
               where (EF.Functions.DateDiffMinute(a.StartTime, fdate) >= 0) &&
                     (EF.Functions.DateDiffMinute(a.EndTime, fdate) <= 0) &&
                     a.driver == driver
               select new { a.ProTaskId, a.StartTime, a.EndTime, a.TaskName, e.FirstName }).ToList();

2) Build the assignee names once (place it before the loop, keyed by ProTaskId). This reproduces the legacy `(join Employees ... where Status=="Assigned" && chkStatus==active).Distinct().Select(FirstName)`:

   var assignedLookup = (from z in db.TaskAssigneds
                         join y in db.Employees on z.EmployeeId equals y.EmployeeId
                         where z.Status == "Assigned" && z.chkStatus == Status.active
                         select new { z.ProTaskId, FirstName = (y.FirstName != null) ? y.FirstName : "" })
                        .Distinct().ToList()
                        .GroupBy(x => x.ProTaskId)
                        .ToDictionary(g => g.Key, g => String.Join(",", g.Select(o => o.FirstName).ToArray()));

   (db.TaskAssigneds.Join(...).Where(...).Distinct().ToList() is itself a top-level standalone query, so it materializes safely; the GroupBy/ToDictionary then runs client-side.)

3) At each consumption site (lines 781, 823, 865) replace
       string ass = String.Join(",", t.AssignedTo.Select(o => o.FirstName).ToArray());
   with
       string ass = assignedLookup.TryGetValue(t.ProTaskId, out var a) ? a : "";

This preserves the exact distinct, comma-joined first-name string (AssignedTo is used for nothing else) while eliminating the untranslatable nested collection projection. Note: this is the only behavior `cald` derives from AssignedTo, so no other call sites are affected.
reasoning:
CONFIRMED. File QuickNetCore/Controllers/ProTaskController.cs, method `cald(long? driver, string taskdate)`, query at lines 748-775.

(a) SERVER-SIDE â€” confirmed. The outer query is `from a in db.ProTasks.Where(o=>o.Branch==1) join e in db.Employees ... select new {...}`, composed directly on DbSets with NO `.ToList()`/`.AsEnumerable()`/`.AsAsyncEnumerable()` boundary before it; it is first enumerated by `.ToList()` at line 775. The `let AssignedTo = (from z in db.TaskAssigneds join y in db.Employees on z.EmployeeId equals y.EmployeeId where z.ProTaskId == a.ProTaskId && z.Status=="Assigned" && z.chkStatus==Status.active select new { FirstName=... }).Distinct().ToList()` is a correlated subquery (correlated to outer `a.ProTaskId`) projected into the outer `select new` at line 772 (`AssignedTo`). It is NOT a group-aggregate over an IGrouping (source is db.TaskAssigneds, a DbSet), NOT a standalone top-level query, and NOT after a materialization boundary. Entity types verified in QuickNetCore/Models/ProTask.cs: TaskAssigned{ProTaskId long, EmployeeId long, Status string, chkStatus Status} and Employee.FirstName.

(b) Would 500 in EF Core 10 â€” confirmed. This is the exact "nested collection / sub-entity projection" class that was already confirmed and FIXED elsewhere in THIS SAME controller during batch 24-26: `GetAllTaskdashexp` (line 1727+) explicitly removed "the nested AssignedTo/TskLead/Updateduser collections" from its EF projection (comment at line 1726/1751: "the nested AssignedTo ... collections are removed here and recomputed CLIENT-SIDE" / "assignees (was the nested AssignedTo collection)") precisely because EF Core 10 cannot translate a non-navigation, manually-join-correlated nested collection embedded in an outer server-side projection. EF Core 10 throws "The LINQ expression ... could not be translated" -> HTTP 500. The `.Distinct()` over an anonymous projection compounds the untranslatability. The `cald` site is the same AssignedTo pattern, just missed because it sits inside a 38-iteration `for` loop rather than in a dashboard method.

PORT REGRESSION â€” confirmed. Legacy EF6 at _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/ProTaskController.cs lines 754-781 is byte-for-byte identical except for the date-diff helper (`DbFunctions.DiffMinutes` -> `EF.Functions.DateDiffMinute`). EF6 executed the nested collection as a separate per-row query (relationship-fixup / N+1); EF Core 10 throws. So this is a faithful port whose only behavioral change is the EF6->EF Core translation regression.

SEVERITY high: the only reachability guard is `a.driver == driver` plus a 30-minute time-slot match. Whenever the selected driver actually has a task in the window â€” i.e. the normal case when the calendar shows any content â€” the query yields rows and 500s on the very first matching loop iteration. AssignedTo is consumed ONLY as `String.Join(",", t.AssignedTo.Select(o=>o.FirstName).ToArray())` at lines 781/823/865, so the fix is output-preserving.

breakClass: DateTime arithmetic helper inside an unmaterialized query: DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays/AddMinutes (plain CLR helpers in Helpers/SendMailShim.cs, NOT registered EF functions) applied to a COLUMN expression inside a server-side LINQ-to-Entities projection or Where. EF6 used DbFunctions.AddDays (canonical -> SQL DATEADD); the port swapped in DbFunctionsCompat.* which EF Core 10 cannot translate, so the query throws 'could not be translated' -> HTTP 500. Fix = EF-native (col).Value.AddDays(n ?? 0) which DOES translate to DATEADD.
why: Inside select new of an unmaterialized query (from a in db.servicereports ...); the .ToList() boundary is at L7041 AFTER this projection, so the AddDays runs server-side. The entire ternary (incl. false branch) must translate; DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays is a CLR helper -> untranslatable -> 500 whenever the man-hour grid is opened. Legacy (archive L7452) DbFunctions.AddDays. Port regression.
reasoning:
I attempted to refute but the claim holds on every axis.

SERVER-SIDE: CONFIRMED. At Controllers/ProTaskController.cs:7028 the query is `(from a in db.servicereports join b ... join c ... where a.protaskid==protaskid select new {... endtime = (a.endtime < a.starttime) ? DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays(a.endtime, 1) : a.endtime ...})`. `db.servicereports` is `DbSet<servicereport>` (ApplicationDbContext.cs:67), a root IQueryable. There is NO `.ToList()`/`.AsEnumerable()`/`.ToArray()` before the `select new`. The materialization boundary is at L7041 (`.ToList().Select(...)`) â€” i.e. AFTER the offending projection. So EF Core 10 must translate the entire `select new`, including the ternary's true branch `DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays(a.endtime, 1)`, into SQL. Not a standalone top-level query, not a group-aggregate over an IGrouping, not commented out (the live `var data = (from a in db.servicereports ...` block at 7028-7051 is real; the commented variants are above/below it).

WOULD 500: CONFIRMED. DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays is a plain CLR static helper â€” Helpers/SendMailShim.cs:70: `public static DateTime? AddDays(DateTime? date, int? days) => date?.AddDays(days ?? 0);` â€” NOT a registered/translatable EF function. EF Core 10 cannot translate an arbitrary CLR method embedded in a server-side projection and throws InvalidOperationException 'The LINQ expression ... could not be translated', surfacing as HTTP 500. This fires at translation time whenever the man-hour grid is opened, independent of row count (even 0 rows), since translation precedes execution. Severity: high (unconditional 500 on opening the grid).

PORT REGRESSION: CONFIRMED. Legacy _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/ProTaskController.cs:7452 is byte-for-byte the same projection but uses EF6 canonical `DbFunctions.AddDays(a.endtime, 1)`, which EF6 translated to SQL DATEADD and worked. The port swapped the canonical function for the untranslatable CLR shim. Entity types verified: servicereport.starttime and .endtime are both DateTime? (Models/ProTask.cs:34-35), so `.Value.AddDays(1)` is valid.

The proposed fix is correct and output-preserving: the false-branch `a.endtime` keeps the ternary result type as DateTime?, and the true branch only evaluates where `a.endtime < a.starttime` was true (endtime non-null there), exactly mirroring legacy DbFunctions.AddDays semantics; downstream `(o.endtime - o.starttime).Value.Hours/Minutes` arithmetic at 7049-7050 is unaffected. `.Value.AddDays(1)` translates to DATEADD in EF Core 10.
